Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core

Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.util.timer

package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.util.timer
Frame-based timers package for FlixelGDX.

Note that this package does not use libGDX Timer and does not use background threads. All global timer objects are updated in the main game loop.

Usage

  • Schedule work: FlixelTimer.getGlobalManager().start(seconds, callback, loops) or static helpers FlixelTimer.wait(float, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.util.timer.FlixelTimerListener) and FlixelTimer.loop(float, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.util.timer.FlixelTimerListener, int).
  • Scaling: FlixelGame passes elapsed times Flixel.getTimeScale() into FlixelTimerManager.update(float).
  • Lifecycle: Flixel.resetGame() and Flixel.initialize(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelGame) call FlixelTimerManager.cancelAll() so cold restarts do not leak callbacks.
  • Pooled instances: do not store FlixelTimer references across FlixelTimer.cancel() or completion; the manager returns them to an internal Pool.
See Also:

  • Class
    Description
    FlixelTimer
    Frame-based countdown timer.
    FlixelTimerListener
    Callback invoked when a FlixelTimer completes a loop or the whole run.
    FlixelTimerManager
    Owns active FlixelTimer instances and advances them once per frame.
